summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on April 27, 2021, highlighted by the ceremonial swearing-in of new Police Officer Kyle Houck. The board approved numerous police department actions including donations, a detective reassignment, a school resource officer reassignment, and an SRO MOU with Goshen Local Schools. The board also adopted the township strategic plan, approved a lease agreement with Clermont County Commissioners for a radio communications tower behind the fire station, approved zoning text amendments, authorized a Cincinnati Bell phone/data contract saving $10,400 over three years, and discussed sidewalk planning and township branding/logo design.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills
Granted permission to Goshen Lions Club for Flags for Heroes program at fire department with permanent bases
Accepted $25 gift card donation from Mike Elam for Police Department
Accepted $65 gift card donation from Krista Bergen and family for Police Department
Accepted $50 donation from Shannon and Esther Wilder for Police Department
Approved reassignment of Officer McAllister to detective upon completion of field training duties
Accepted resignation of Ryan Saylor effective June 5, 2021
Approved reassignment of Officer Groves from duty officer to school resource officer
Approved SRO Alcorn to attend 2021 National School Safety Conference at no cost to township
Approved SRO MOU with Goshen Local School District for 2021-2022 school year for two SROs
Declared surplus/obsolete items for sale on GovDeals
Accepted donation of four SUVs from Deerfield Township Fire and EMS Department
Authorized fire chief to promote firefighter paramedic Todd Estep to lieutenant effective/retroactive to April 25, 2021 with six-month probation
Approved Memorial Day festivities on May 31 coordinated by Goshen Chamber of Commerce
Adopted the Goshen Township Strategic Plan
Authorized 36-month contract with Cincinnati Bell for phone, data, and TV services
Decreased appropriations in zoning budget by $35 for housekeeping
Approved Resolution R008-2021 modifying transient vendors/solicitors/peddlers registration from police to zoning department
Approved Resolution R010-2021 modifying zoning resolution text amendments (Articles 7.01 C2A, C2B, C2C, and C3)
Authorized township administrator to sign quit claim deed for vacating portion of East Huntley Road
Approved Resolution R009-2021 lease agreement with Clermont County for radio communications antenna tower behind fire station at $1 lease term
Entered executive session to discuss police department vacancies; no decisions made
Adjourned regular meeting
action_items: Steve Pegram and Sherell Allgaier to finalize and post job ad for administrative assistant in paper and Facebook within 1-2 weeks
Chief Rose to bring new police patch design for formal adoption at upcoming trustees meeting
Chad Meadows to complete concrete pouring, landscaping, and preparation of columbarium shelter for Memorial Day
Plan dedication ceremony for columbarium with plaque recognizing builders, separate from Memorial Day
Schedule special meeting on May 18 for township branding/logo discussion and adoption
Steve Pegram to check with consultant Eric regarding ownership/rights of existing logo design
Steve Pegram to prepare research presentation of 100+ Ohio township logos for branding meeting
Chad Meadows to trench electric to columbarium for lighting
Harry Holbert to obtain signatures on zoning resolution and quit claim deed originals

attendees: Becca Rose (Goshen Lions Club / Wayne Township resident)
Donna Rosick (Citizens Police Academy Executive Board)
Alyssa Hundley (new part-time admin assistant, mentioned)
notes: The meeting had significant audio/video issues with repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.' placeholder text appearing throughout the transcript, likely due to automated transcription errors or signal drops. The executive session lasted from 8:43 PM to 9:05 PM and discussed police department vacancies with no decisions made. The transcript also contained some garbled/nonsensical text segments. Trustee Hauserman was congratulated on being elected Vice President of the Clermont County Trustee Association. A special meeting was planned for May 18 specifically to finalize the township branding/logo. The board discussed frustration with limited township funding compared to cities receiving large COVID stimulus allocations.
title: BOT 4.27.21
